narration_text: Now we'll see how a queue, or buffer, works in the context of a preceding link. Look at the image on the slide, notice how the packets waiting to be transmitted are stored in a buffer, essentially a waiting area. This buffer has a finite capacity, meaning it can only hold a certain number of packets before it becomes full. I want you to focus on the visual representation of this concept, observe how the packets are arranged and how the buffer operates. This will help you understand the relationship between the queue and the link, and how it affects the transmission process. Let's think about this together, and then we'll move on to explore how packet loss occurs when the buffer is full.
